evals.prompt: Review the docs/ folder — 6 markdown files covering install, config, deployment, and API usage.
evals.expected_output: Report with scope statement, map, findings by tier, set-level checks, and a recommended order of work. No files modified.
assertions: States what was included and excluded from scope
Says whether the doc map was declared or inferred
Reviews inline without spawning sub-agents at this size
Groups findings as blocking / warning / suggestion
Every finding cites a path and a heading or line
Ends with a recommended order of work, not just a finding list
Modifies no files

evals.prompt: Review these docs. getting-started.md says the default port is 8080; reference/config.md says 3000.
evals.expected_output: Divergent duplication raised as blocking, with a determination of which is correct.
assertions: Classifies this as divergent duplication, not verbatim duplication
Rates it blocking
States which value is correct and the basis for that conclusion, or says explicitly it could not be established
Does not merely report that the two documents disagree

evals.prompt: Review the docs. The quickstart repeats the default port and the auth flow summary, then links to the reference pages for full detail.
evals.expected_output: Restatement recognised as legitimate and not flagged.
assertions: Does NOT flag the quickstart repetition as duplication
Applies the restatement test — repetition that points at a source of truth is not a finding
Does not suggest removing orienting summaries from the quickstart

evals.expected_output: Per-document pass batched across agents; cross-document passes done by the parent with the whole set in view.
assertions: Batches the per-document quality pass across document-quality-reviewer agents
Does NOT parallelise boundaries, duplication, or consistency checks
States the reason cross-document work is not batched, or simply performs it centrally
Reports set-wide patterns once with examples and a count rather than one finding per instance

evals.prompt: Review docs/. Two documents both look like the authority on deployment, and one page is linked from nothing.
evals.expected_output: Contested boundary and orphan both raised; boundary sequenced first in the recommended order.
assertions: Raises the contested boundary between the two deployment documents
Raises the orphan under set-level navigation
Suggests one document owns the procedure and the other links to it
Orders boundary resolution before downstream consistency fixes

evals.prompt: Review the docs. Every page uses sentence-case headings and second person, which is not my preference.
evals.expected_output: Consistent house style not raised as a finding.
assertions: Does NOT flag consistently-applied house style as an error
Would flag only inconsistent application of the convention
Does not impose an external documentation framework on the set
